proven.claim: Claim: When runDiff is entered, the log must contain the VERSION string and the reference `ref`.
proven.smt2:
; LINE: 267
; PRINCIPLE: P1 – Precondition Propagation
; Claim: When runDiff is entered, the log must contain the VERSION string and the reference `ref`.
;   console.log(`neurallog v${VERSION} — proof diff against ${ref}`)
;   ⇒  VERSION is a non‑empty string and ref is a non‑empty string.
; PROVEN: we show that the negation (VERSION empty ∨ ref empty) is impossible.

(declare-const VERSION String)           ; from module constant
(declare-const ref String)               ; computed from args (may be default)

; The code guarantees both strings are non‑empty:
;   - VERSION is a literal "0.3.0" in the source → non‑empty.
;   - ref is either the user‑supplied argument or the default "HEAD~1" → non‑empty.
(assert (> (str.len VERSION) 0))
(assert (> (str.len ref) 0))

; Negation of the claim: either VERSION empty or ref empty.
(assert (or (= (str.len VERSION) 0) (= (str.len ref) 0)))

(check-sat)   ; expected: unsat
